details: Digital merch CSV (237 rows) landed via Telegram. Autopilot Luffy/Nami/Zoro/Sanji/Jonah chain queued to generate slugs, templates, QA, and deployment notes.
Cloned ValueCell-ai/ClawX and installed PNPM dependencies so the repo is build-ready for any automation tasks or skill integrations.
Built a dedicated cyberpunk landing page in DmerchPortal that highlights the 237-item catalog and links directly to https://paymentportal.digitalmerchs.store with the secure access reminder.
Rad Approval tab now appears on the Mission Control sidebar so you can always open the latest log summary without extra requests.
DmerchPortal redeployed to Vercel via CLI; https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app now serves the payment-gated landing page.
Removed Mission Control references and added richer category breakdowns in the DmerchPortal landing page; build verified locally.
Redeployed DmerchPortal (with categorization updates) to Vercel; https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app remains live and updated.
Landing page now lets buyers click each category card to open a searchable list of all matching products; redeployed to keep https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing fresh.
Category cards now populate inline product grids with graphics and descriptions (no pop-up), redeployed to keep https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing in sync with the buyer experience.

Inline cart now copies product names and persists in localStorage, plus a quick 'Go to payment portal with cart' link feeds the portal with the selected products. Redeployed to https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing.
Updated landing page buttons to just 'Add to cart' (copy is still stored) and redeployed so https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing matches the buyer flow.
Shopping cart button now simply reads 'Checkout' while still passing the selected products to https://paymentportal.digitalmerchs.store/; redeployed landing page with this tweak.
Rebuilt and redeployed Mission Control app to clear the 404; production now at https://mission-control-ivory-nine.vercel.app with the Rad Approval workflow and new task board.
Removed the Rad Approval tab and route from Mission Control since it is no longer needed; navigation now only includes core dashboards and the cart explorer elements remain logged here.
Converted the landing page into a game catalog with official gameplay reels, new headings, and Checkout cart flow; deployed on https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing.
Pushed the latest DmerchPortal game catalog landing to https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing with the Checkout flow.
Updated the landing page copy to English to match the Leadpages-style reference and redeployed to https://dmerch-portal.vercel.app/landing.
